acknowledgedFindings.reason: Demo file with intentional HIPAA violations used to showcase the scanner (hardcoded SSN, TLS validation disabled, hardcoded password, CORS wildcard, etc.). Not production code.
Marketing copy describes weak crypto algorithms ("MD5 and DES") as detection examples — text content, not code.
Marketing copy mentions "auth bypass" detection — text content, not code.

Rule fires on every file importing Supabase regardless of actual MFA implementation. False positive while regex is improved. Tracked upstream: https://github.com/Francosimon53/verification-layer/issues/32
Rule fires on createClient patterns regardless of session config — false positive when using Supabase managed sessions. Tracked upstream: https://github.com/Francosimon53/verification-layer/issues/33
Static landing page repo with auth via Supabase — no application logging framework needed. Revisit when adding server-side application logic.
Vulnerability scanning IS configured: this scanner runs on cron + Dependabot enablement is planned. False negative on the rule's detection of CI configuration.
